data.title: 908 Devices to Report First Quarter 2026 Financial Results on May 6, 2026
data.description:
BURLINGTON, Mass.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--May 01, 2026--

908 Devices Inc. , a core small-cap growth company focused on purpose-built handheld chemical analysis tools for vital health, safety and defense tech applications, announced it will report financial results for the first quarter 2026 before market open on Wednesday, May 6, 2026. Company management will webcast a corresponding conference call beginning at 8:30 a.m. Eastern Time.

Live audio of the webcast will be available on the "Investors" section of the company website at: www.908devices.com. The webcast will be archived and available for replay within 24 hours after the event.

About 908 Devices

908 Devices is revolutionizing chemical analysis with its simple handheld devices, addressing life-altering applications. The Company's devices are used at the point-of-need to interrogate unknown and invisible materials and provide quick, actionable answers in vital health, safety and defense tech applications, addressing the fentanyl and illicit drug crisis, toxic carcinogen exposure, and global security threats. The Company designs and manufactures innovative products that bring together the power of complementary analytical technologies, software automation, and machine learning. For more information, visit www.908devices.com.

View source version on businesswire.com: https://www.businesswire.com/news/home/20260501692729/en/

CONTACT: IR & Media

Barbara Russo

IR@908devices.com

data.title: Datavault AI to Buy CyberCatch For C$136.8 million
data.description:
By Nicholas G. Miller

Datavault AI has signed a letter of intent to acquire cybersecurity company CyberCatch in a deal worth C$136.8 million (US$100.8 million).

Datavault will provide 49.9 million newly issued shares at a value of C$5.11 per CyberCatch share. Datavault AI stockholders will hold 92.48% of the combined company with CyberCatch shareholders owning the rest.

Datavault said adding CyberCatch's AI cybersecurity platform will allow it to provide important risk-mitigation and compliance for its customers and better prepare for a quantum era of cybersecurity.

The companies have agreed to negotiate a definitive agreement during a 45-day mutual exclusivity period.

Write to Nicholas G. Miller at nicholas.miller@wsj.com

data.title: Meta Platforms Signals More Layoffs Amid Rising AI Costs
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META, Financials) is preparing for deeper workforce cuts as it ramps up spending on artificial intelligence.

CEO Mark Zuckerberg told employees that the company is reallocating resources toward compute infrastructure, a shift that is forcing reductions elsewhere including headcount.

We have two major cost centers: infrastructure and people, Zuckerberg said, adding that higher investment in AI means less capital available for staffing. Meta plans to lay off about 10% of its workforce starting May 20 and left the door open to more cuts later this year.

The comments come as the company reorganizes around an AI-native structure and pushes to build autonomous AI agents. While Zuckerberg said efficiency tools are not directly driving layoffs, he acknowledged uncertainty about how the transition will unfold.

The shift has seen internal backlash from employees, underscoring the friction between Meta's aggressive AI push and the stability of its personnel. Investors now are focusing on how greater AI spending will effect margins and the long term.

data.title: Gold Nears $4,630 As Dollar Retreat Offsets Iran War Inflation Risks
data.description:
Gold (GLD) has regained some ground after the US dollar pulled back sharply, giving investors a fresh reason to revisit bullion even as inflation risks from the Iran war continue to pressure the broader setup. Bullion traded near $4,630 an ounce in early trading after gaining 1.5% in the previous session, while spot gold rose 0.3% to $4,632.84 an ounce at 7:43 a.m. in Singapore. The move followed a sharp rally in the yen, which surged more than 2% on Thursday for its biggest gain in three years after the Nikkei newspaper reported that Japan's government bought yen and sold dollars. That dollar weakness matters for gold because bullion is priced in the US currency, meaning a softer greenback can make the metal more attractive to buyers.

The rebound comes after a difficult stretch for gold, which had fallen for three straight days before rallying late Thursday. The pressure has been tied to the nine-week Middle East conflict, which has kept the Strait of Hormuz effectively closed to shipping, with no progress in peace talks. US President Donald Trump said he is sticking with a naval blockade of Iranian ports, while Iran's supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei vowed to retain the country's nuclear technologies and signaled Tehran would keep control of the strait. Oil held its second weekly gain, and the energy-supply shock has added to inflation risks, possibly making it more likely that central banks keep rates steady for longer or even move toward hikes. That could be a drag for non-yielding bullion, which has lost about 12% since the conflict began at the end of February.

Still, the investment case for gold has not been knocked out. Citigroup analysts including Kenny Hu said selling pressure could remain strong in the very near term because of Middle East uncertainty, especially if higher gold prices come alongside another wave of equity-market correction. At the same time, they said gold could eventually outshine as a haven asset, supported by heightened geopolitical uncertainty and rising stagflation risks. The latest producer-funded World Gold Council data also showed that central banks added gold holdings in the first quarter at the fastest pace in more than a year, with the price slump encouraging buying that more than offset sales by a handful of institutions. Silver rose 0.9% to $74.44 an ounce, while platinum and palladium also advanced, leaving precious metals broadly firmer as investors weighed dollar weakness against the inflation shock from the Iran war.

data.title: Nissan Cancels Two US Electric SUVs As EV Demand Weakens
data.description:
Nissan Motor Co. (NSANY) is pulling back from a key piece of its US electric-vehicle strategy as demand among American consumers for all-electric vehicles continues to soften. The Japanese automaker is canceling plans to build two fully electric SUVs at its Canton, Mississippi factory, a decision relayed Thursday to dealers and parts suppliers in the US. For investors, the move could be read as another signal that automakers are being forced to rethink how quickly they commit capital to fully electric models in a market where demand may no longer support the same level of urgency.

The cancellation comes as part of a broader recalibration of Nissan's product strategy, with the company seeking to conserve cash while adjusting its production priorities. Nissan had previously frozen plans to begin EV manufacturing at the Canton plant, and this latest decision suggests management is possibly leaning toward a more measured approach rather than pushing ahead with all-electric SUVs before demand becomes more supportive. Instead, the company expects to build a V6 engine-powered hybrid version of its Xterra SUV at the Mississippi facility.

This shift does not suggest Nissan is walking away from electrification, but it does show the company may be prioritizing flexibility, cash preservation, and products that better match current US consumer appetite. The investor takeaway is that hybrids could continue gaining strategic relevance as fully electric demand remains uneven in the American market. Nissan's Canton reset now raises a key question for shareholders: whether a slower, more selective EV rollout could help protect financial flexibility while keeping the company positioned for the next phase of the auto industry's transition.

data.title: Goldman revamps Apple stock price target for rest of 2026
data.description:
Goldman Sachs raised its price target on Apple to $340 from $330 while maintaining a buy rating, citing steady demand across key product lines and improving cost discipline.

Apple has continued to benefit from solid momentum in iPhone, Mac, and services segments. The company reported quarterly revenue growth of 17%, though supply constraints tied to advanced semiconductor production limited further upside. Demand appears to be running ahead of available supply, particularly for newer devices.

Margins also improved, with Apple posting gross margin near 49%, supported by favorable product mix despite rising component costs. The company expects similar supply challenges in the current quarter but indicated that underlying demand trends remain intact.

Apple is also pursuing additional capital returns, increasing its share repurchase authorization by $100 billion. Analysts note that continued strength in hardware and services, along with demand tied to AI-related workloads, may help offset near-term cost pressures.

data.title: Tesla Revenue From Musk-Linked Companies Tops $500M
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA, Financials) produced over $500 million in revenue last year via deals with companies related to CEO Elon Musk, according to an amended annual filing.

Most of that sum came from dealings with xAI, which accounted for over $430.1 million. The revelation shows how Musk's economic empire is getting further enmeshed across Tesla, xAI and other ventures.

It's not just the quantity of the revenue that is the worry for investors, but the governance questions it could raise. Transactions with related parties may be subject to greater scrutiny as they include companies that share the same executive.

The filing comes as Tesla continues to push forward on its AI objectives but navigating the pressure of competition, profitability and shareholder expectations.

Investors will be looking for additional detail from Tesla on these deals and how they fit into the company's overall AI strategy.

data.title: SanDisk Crushes Earnings Estimate -- Why the Stock Is Tumbling
data.description:
SanDisk shares fell 5% on early Friday trading even after the company posted stronger-than-expected fiscal third-quarter results, as investors appeared to lock in gains following a prolonged rally tied to artificial intelligence demand.

SanDisk reported fiscal Q3 revenue of $5.95 billion, more than double from a year earlier and above expectations. Adjusted earnings came in at $23.41 per share, compared with a loss in the prior-year period, reflecting improved pricing and a shift toward higher-margin data center demand.

SanDisk said margins expanded sharply during the quarter, supported by stronger pricing and product mix. The company also outlined a transition toward longer-term customer agreements, which may provide more predictable revenue streams. For the fourth quarter, SanDisk expects revenue between $7.75 billion and $8.25 billion, with adjusted earnings projected at $30 to $33 per share.

Analysts responded by raising price targets, citing continued demand for memory tied to AI workloads. They also pointed to multi-year contracts and tighter supply conditions as potential supports for pricing through 2026.

data.title: Apple Tops Q2 Estimates on Surging iPhone and China Sales
data.description:
Apple reported second-quarter results Thursday that topped Wall Street estimates on both profit and revenue, helped by stronger iPhone demand and solid sales in China.

Apple posted earnings per share of $2.01 on revenue of $111.2 billion, compared with $1.65 and $95.35 billion a year earlier. iPhone sales rose to $56.99 billion, while Services brought in $30.97 billion, both ahead of forecasts.

Chief Executive Tim Cook said demand for the iPhone 17 lineup was strong. Apple also recorded $8.39 billion in Mac revenue, helped by the Mac mini and the new $599 MacBook Neo, which have drawn interest from developers building AI tools.

Apple said Greater China revenue improved to $20.49 billion, above analyst expectations. The report also came as Apple prepared for Cook to step down in September, with hardware chief John Ternus set to succeed him. The company said rising memory prices tied to AI data center demand may pressure margins.

data.title: Microsoft: Heavy CapEx, Clear AI Payoff Path
data.description:
Microsoft's latest earnings report had a big focus on how the company is monetising artificial intelligence, with growth in revenue, cloud services, and AI adoption figures.

Revenue grew 18% year-on-year to $82.9 billion, while adjusted operating income grew 20% to $38.4 billion. The company's operating margin widened to some 46%, despite higher investment in AI technology, showing continued pricing and productivity improvements.

The company's cloud unit, Azure, continued to grow strongly, with revenue up 40% year-on-year. Managers indicated the market is growing faster than capacity, with more raw material needed. It expects this growth to continue in the coming quarters as capacity increases.

Microsoft's AI business passed $37 billion on an annualized run rate (more than doubling year-on-year). Active paid seats of Copilot topped 20 million, and usage grew. Microsoft is furthering its shift from a subscription to a mixed subscription and usage-based business model to monetize more value from its installed base.

Investment highlighted in cash flows. Cash flow from operations was up 26% to $46.7 billion, but free cash flow declined to $15.8 billion owing to capex of over $30 billion this quarter. This was largely in the form of shorter-lived assets to enable AI inference, such as GPUs and CPUs.

Declared estimated remaining performance obligations (RPO) for the commercial segment were $627 billion, giving an indication of the potential future revenues. Investments were linked to this backlog, as executives noted they plan to invest to address demand.

This is an inflection point, as investments in infrastructure have a negative impact on free cash flow in the short term, but build capacity for the future of AI.

data.title: U.K. Manufacturing PMI Upgrade Suggests Economy Resilient Amid War — Market Talk
data.description: The upward revision to April's U.K. manufacturing PMI tallies with other indicators showing the economy is holding up well amid surging energy prices, Pantheon Macroeconomics' Rob Wood and Elliott Jordan-Doak say in a note. April's manufacturing PMI was 53.7, up from an initial estimate of 53.6 and from 51.0 in March. That suggests sentiment improved between the end of the flash survey period on April 21 and the final survey end on April 27, despite oil prices surging, the economists say. New orders, export orders, and employment all improved. Though some of the demand reflects firms getting ahead of expected supply disruptions, the improvement could prompt Pantheon to lift its expectation for zero second-quarter growth, they say. (edward.frankl@wsj.com)

data.title: SETO Holdings Subsidiary Shinju Japanese Whisky Joins FullScale Marketplace to Expand Creator-Led Commerce and Digital Distribution
data.description:
Groundbreaking Japanese whisky brand now accessible to creators through a performance-driven digital commerce platform

WASHINGTON, DC / ACCESS Newswire / May 1, 2026 / Seto Holdings, Inc. (OTCID:SETO) ("SETO" or the "Company"), a publicly traded, vertically integrated beverage platform, today announced that its subsidiary, Shinju Spirits, Inc., the company behind Shinju Japanese Whisky, has entered into a strategic partnership with FullScale, a digital engagement and creator commerce platform designed to connect premium brands with high-performing creators.

Through this partnership, Shinju becomes an active brand partner within the FullScale Marketplace, enabling creators to seamlessly integrate the brand into authentic content, drive measurable engagement, and participate directly in revenue generation.

This collaboration represents a strategic expansion of Shinju's digital and direct-to-consumer capabilities, aligning with broader initiatives under SETO Holdings, Inc. to build a vertically integrated, technology-enabled beverage platform.

A Scalable "Creators Meet Commerce" Model

FullScale's platform is built around a performance-based ecosystem where creators and brands collaborate through trackable campaigns, unlocking new distribution channels beyond traditional retail and on-premise models.

By joining the marketplace, Shinju enables:

  • Authentic brand placement across creator-driven content ecosystems

  • Performance-based revenue participation tied to measurable outcomes

  • Scalable digital distribution aligned with evolving consumer behavior

  • Direct engagement with next-generation whisky consumers

Leadership Perspectives

"Digital engagement is no longer a marketing layer-it's a core distribution channel," said Janon Costley, Founder and CEO of Shinju Spirits, Inc. "This partnership with FullScale positions Shinju at the intersection of culture, commerce, and content - where modern spirits brands are built and scaled."

Strategic Implications

This partnership reinforces Shinju's positioning within a broader shift toward:

  • Creator-led commerce as a distribution channel

  • Performance marketing tied directly to revenue

  • Platform-driven brand scaling beyond legacy retail constraints

As consumer discovery continues to migrate toward digital ecosystems, Shinju's integration into FullScale provides a framework for repeatable, data-driven growth across multiple markets.

This announcement follows the Company's previously disclosed digital strategy initiative, reinforcing SETO's focus on building technology-enabled distribution and consumer engagement capabilities across its brand portfolio. The Company expects to continue expanding its digital infrastructure and brand activation capabilities through additional platform development and brand launches.

About FullScale

FullScale is a digital marketplace that connects premium brands with vetted creators to drive authentic engagement, measurable performance, and revenue-generating brand partnerships. The platform enables scalable creator-led commerce through integrated campaign management, analytics, and monetization tools.

About Shinju Japanese Whisky

Shinju Whisky is a groundbreaking Japanese whisky produced from pristine alpine water , double distilled and blended to deliver a smooth, approachable profile for a global audience.

As the first Black-founded and led Japanese Whisky brand, Shinju is focused on expanding access, redefining norms by engaging and inviting a more diverse and modern whisky consumer to the category.

The brand is currently available in major retailers such as Total Wine & More, BevMax, as well as directly through the brand's e-commerce platform, www.shinjuwhisky.com.

About SETO Holdings, Inc.

A Gateway for Global Spirits, Innovation & Shareholder Value

SETO Holdings, Inc. is a publicly traded (OTCID:SETO), vertically integrated total beverage platform redefining the future of premium beverages through cultural relevance, vertical integration, and digital innovation. Guided by its mission to "Own the Spirit", SETO merges production, distribution, marketing, technology, and capital markets into a unified engine for scalable brand growth for under-valued companies and founders.

Forward-Looking Statements

This press release contains forward-looking statements within the meaning of applicable securities laws. These statements reflect current expectations regarding future events and involve risks and uncertainties that may cause actual results to differ materially. Factors that could cause such differences include, but are not limited to, market conditions, consumer demand, execution of strategic initiatives, and other risks detailed in public filings. Shinju Spirits, Inc. undertakes no obligation to update any forward-looking statements.

Media & Partnership Inquiries

IR@seto-holdings.com

info@shinjuwhisky.com

Websites

Shinju - www.Shinjuwhisky.com

FullScale - gofullscale.co

Seto Holdings, Inc - www.seto-holdings.com

SOURCE: SETO Holdings, Inc.

data.title: Sable CEO Gets $76 Million Pay Package Despite No 2025 Crude Sales
data.description:
Sable Offshore Corp. is facing renewed investor scrutiny after CEO Jim Flores received a $76 million compensation package in 2025, even though the California oil driller sold no crude during the year. According to a Thursday filing with federal regulators, the bulk of Flores's pay came from more than $69 million in stock awards, alongside a $1.3 million salary and a $3.9 million bonus. The jump was striking compared with 2024, when Flores earned $1.7 million and received no stock awards, possibly putting the company's executive-pay structure under a brighter market spotlight.

The award landed during a difficult year for Sable, whose shares fell 61% in 2025 as the company's years-long effort to restart oil production from rigs off the California coast was slowed by local and state-level opposition. The company was also probed by federal investigators after Hunterbrook Media reported that Sable selectively disclosed information to investors, including pro golfer Phil Mickelson. Flores's compensation also exceeded the 2025 pay of Exxon Mobil CEO Darren Woods, who earned $32 million, and Chevron Corp. CEO Mike Wirth, who earned $26.8 million, even though those two energy majors pumped almost 9 million barrels of oil a day over the year.

The story has since taken a sharper turn, with Sable now producing and selling about 50,000 barrels of oil daily after President Donald Trump invoked a war-time law in March and directed the company to begin pumping, overriding opposition from California. The company also paid $540,000 to Holland & Knight, a law firm with close ties to the White House, in the first three months of 2026, the same period in which Trump used Cold War-era powers to clear the way for production. For investors, the setup could be difficult to ignore: Sable awarded one of the largest oil-and-gas CEO pay packages in the US during a year with no crude sales, then entered 2026 with federal intervention possibly reshaping its production outlook.

data.title: More states join legal challenge to Nexstar, Tegna merger
data.description:
By Daniel Wiessner and David Shepardson

Five more U.S. states are joining an antitrust lawsuit challenging Nexstar's acquisition of rival broadcaster Tegna after a ‌judge temporarily blocked the deal from proceeding, according to California's attorney general.

Attorney General Rob Bonta, a Democrat whose office joined seven other states in suing over the $6.2 billion deal in March, said on Thursday that Massachusetts, Vermont, and his Republican counterparts in Indiana, Kansas, and Pennsylvania were joining the case.

"These misguided attorneys general are strangling local journalism," Nexstar said, arguing that the real drivers of local news decline are "the unchecked rise of Big Tech platforms, the spread of misinformation on social media, and the economic pressures that have already led to widespread newsroom closures."

Separately, Republican Ohio Attorney General Dave Yost said on Thursday that he struck a deal with Nexstar to maintain the independence of two local stations in Columbus and Cleveland where the company would own two affiliates following the merger. The deal requires maintaining separate news teams at each station and preserving existing levels of local programming through 2030.

U.S. District Judge Troy Nunley in Sacramento said in an April 17 ruling that the plaintiffs were likely to succeed in their ​claims that the deal will substantially lessen competition in dozens of ​local television markets.

The court’s order bars Nexstar from consolidating its operations with ⁠Tegna pending further litigation, but does not unwind the transaction.

The deal quickly closed after the ​Justice Department and the Federal Communications Commission approved it on March 19, which Nexstar ​noted in a statement announcing its appeal of Nunley's decision.

The deal would create the largest broadcast station group ​in the ⁠United States, reaching 80% of households. The states have argued that the deal would result in lost jobs, increased cable bills and "significantly impact the delivery of news and other ⁠media content ​to Americans nationwide."

Nexstar has ​said its deal with Tegna will strengthen local stations and support investment in local journalism.
